title: Human Interface Architecture Foundation

nodes.name: Human Interface
nodes.description: Defines governed human interaction with Ageix for review, approval, feedback, manual workflow initiation, notifications, and future human-facing entry points. This domain does not implement UI functionality and does not own Ageix state.

nodes.name: Interaction Shell
nodes.description: Defines the boundary for Open WebUI and future human-facing shells. Interface shells display Ageix context and collect human input but remain non-authoritative.

nodes.name: Governed Interaction Adapter
nodes.description: Defines the adapter boundary between human-facing shells and governed Ageix capabilities. State-changing actions must route through project-scoped governed capability execution.

nodes.name: Decision Review
nodes.description: Defines human-facing review concepts for proposals, architecture decisions, evidence, validation outcomes, waivers, approvals, and feedback while preserving existing governance paths.

nodes.name: Notification Intake
nodes.description: Defines future notification and lightweight approval-link intake boundaries without introducing mobile implementation or new authority paths.

relationships.description: Human Interface is a first-level Ageix architecture domain.
Human-facing actions enter Ageix through governed capability surfaces rather than direct mutation.
Review, approval, waiver, and feedback flows preserve existing governance semantics.
Human interaction must remain project-scoped and session-aware.
Human interfaces must not bypass admission, identity, authorization, or trust-boundary checks.
Open WebUI and future web shells are presentation and input surfaces only.

responsibilities: Present governed Ageix context to humans for review and action.
Collect human approvals, feedback, decisions, and manual workflow initiation intent.
Route state-changing requests through governed project-scoped Ageix capabilities.
Preserve evidence, validation, proposal, architecture, worker, and audit traceability.
Define interface boundaries for Open WebUI and future human-facing shells without implementing them.
invariants: Open WebUI is a shell only.
Ageix remains the system of record.
No direct UI-to-worker mutation path is introduced.
All state-changing UI actions route through governed Ageix capabilities.
Human Interface does not weaken project-scoped operations.
